card.intuition: Ensuring that a website can be fully operated using only a keyboard, which is essential for users with motor disabilities and for power users.
card.analogy: A physical building without stairs: if you only have an elevator (Mouse), someone whose elevator is broken (Mouse broken or can't use it) can't get to the second floor. Keyboard navigation (The Stairs) ensures everyone has an alternative way to reach every 'room' in the app.
when_to_use: Building accessible forms and navigation menus
Implementing 'Skip to Content' links for screen reader users
Managing focus when a modal or sidebar opens
gotchas: Never remove the 'Focus Outline' (`outline: none`) without providing a clearly visible alternative style. This makes the site 'invisible' for keyboard users
The 'Tab Order' should follow the logical visual layout of the page (top-to-bottom, left-to-right)

steps.label: Tab order
steps.annotation: Tab key moves focus through interactive elements in DOM order.

steps.label: Focus ring
steps.annotation: Focused element shows visible focus indicator via :focus-visible.

steps.label: Trap focus
steps.annotation: Dialog traps focus inside — Tab wraps, Escape closes.

steps.label: Arrow keys
steps.annotation: Composite widgets (menu, tabs) handle arrow keys internally per ARIA pattern.

steps.label: Skip links
steps.annotation: "Skip to main" link lets keyboard users bypass repeated navigation.

exercise.prompt: Identify the HTML attribute used to determine the order in which elements are focused when pressing the 'Tab' key.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "tabindex"
assert check("tabindex")
print("PASS")
